title: Will Javokhir Sindarov win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament?
description:
This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament scheduled for March 29 - April 16, 2026.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament per the rules of the FIDE, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament is cancelled, or postponed after April 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIDE; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

case.title: Will Javokhir Sindarov win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament?
case.description:
This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament scheduled for March 29 - April 16, 2026.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament per the rules of the FIDE, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament is cancelled, or postponed after April 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIDE; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

initial_forecast.question: Will Javokhir Sindarov win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament?
initial_forecast.time_horizon_label: Final rounds and any official FIDE result declaration
initial_forecast.rationale_summary: Sindarov still looks very likely to win because credible late-stage reporting says he leads by two points with two rounds left and has at least a playoff guaranteed, but without a clean official FIDE standings capture the market's 95% price is too rich for fresh action.

latest_forecast.question: Will Javokhir Sindarov win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament?
latest_forecast.time_horizon_label: Through remaining rounds, official FIDE standings updates, and final winner declaration
latest_forecast.rationale_summary: Sindarov still looks overwhelmingly likely to win, but because the 99.05% market price is not matched by a clean official FIDE crosstable or winner confirmation and the tournament remained live at analysis time, the disciplined output is watch-only and needs-more-research rather than chasing near-certainty.

forecast_rows.question: Will Javokhir Sindarov win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament?
forecast_rows.time_horizon_label: Final rounds and any official FIDE result declaration
forecast_rows.rationale_summary: Sindarov still looks very likely to win because credible late-stage reporting says he leads by two points with two rounds left and has at least a playoff guaranteed, but without a clean official FIDE standings capture the market's 95% price is too rich for fresh action.

forecast_rows.question: Will Javokhir Sindarov win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament?
forecast_rows.time_horizon_label: Through remaining rounds, official FIDE standings updates, and final winner declaration
forecast_rows.rationale_summary: Sindarov still looks overwhelmingly likely to win, but because the 99.05% market price is not matched by a clean official FIDE crosstable or winner confirmation and the tournament remained live at analysis time, the disciplined output is watch-only and needs-more-research rather than chasing near-certainty.

key_assumptions: The checked 9/12 after-round-12 standings snapshot is materially accurate.
Standard Candidates rules and first-place tie-break procedures will govern the final result.
No extraordinary withdrawal, annulment, or reporting error intervenes before official FIDE confirmation.
strongest_supports: Sindarov was listed on 9/12 after 12 rounds with nearest chaser on 7/12 and only two rounds left.
A two-point lead that late in an 8-player Candidates is structurally massive and usually converts.
Even if caught, an additional adverse tie-break path may still be needed for him to lose.
strongest_disconfirmers: The tournament is not over yet: two rounds and possible tie-breaks remain.
Official FIDE information is the governing source, and a clean official standings page was not directly captured in this run.
Secondary scoreboard sources can be stale or wrong in edge cases.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: High: the case is late-stage with only two rounds left, so small new official updates can move the remaining residual risk quickly.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: Settlement mechanics are clear from contract language, but current-score evidence was mainly secondary/contextual rather than two clean independent primary captures.

key_assumptions: Official FIDE round-12 standings are the governing current-state source.
Round 13 versus Giri is the only major remaining reopening catalyst.
Tie-for-first or playoff mechanics remain possible enough to keep the estimate below the market.
strongest_supports: FIDE standings after round 12 show Sindarov on 9/12, two points clear with two rounds left.
Official round 9-12 reporting shows challengers repeatedly failing to cut materially into the lead.
Remaining schedule concentrates risk into one obvious node rather than broad field uncertainty.
strongest_disconfirmers: Giri is still second and gets the direct head-to-head chance in round 13.
A tie-for-first / playoff path could still matter, so classical lead is not identical to settled market resolution.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: This is almost entirely a next-catalyst case now: round 13 versus Giri is the main live event that can still move the market materially before final resolution.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: High on current-state evidence because FIDE is both official organizer and named resolution source; moderate on structural cross-check because the independent contextual verification was Wikipedia rather than directly parsed official regulations text.

key_assumptions: Sindarov holds a near-decisive late-stage lead rather than a merely narrow one.
Secondary contextual reporting on his dominant score after 12 rounds is directionally accurate.
No operational or rules issue removes him from contention before formal resolution.
strongest_supports: Polymarket price of 0.9905 implies traders see the tournament as almost decided.
FIDE official site confirms the event is live at round 13 with Sindarov featured in coverage.
Contextual reporting says Sindarov had six wins and six draws through 12 games and was favored with two rounds remaining.
strongest_disconfirmers: No clean official FIDE crosstable or exact tie-break math was directly captured in-tool.
Two rounds remaining still leaves some nontrivial upset paths compared with a 99% price.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: Very high because this is a late-stage live tournament market and calibration can move sharply on each round result.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: Good on contract/source-of-truth and live-event verification, only moderate on exact standings auditability.

key_assumptions: FIDE reporting accurately reflects the late-stage standings and live race state.
The market contract resolves on official winner declaration rather than dominant in-tournament position.
Residual upset, tiebreak, or operational risk remains nonzero with rounds still left to play.
strongest_supports: FIDE round reports show Sindarov maintaining a large lead from rounds 9 through 12.
Round 12 leaves the nearest challenger in a must-win spot rather than meaningfully improving comeback odds.
Polymarket itself frames Sindarov as around 99.2% implied after round 12 because of the dominant tournament state.
strongest_disconfirmers: FIDE still treated round 13 as live and meaningful, so the event was not formally settled yet.
A remaining loss, tie/tiebreak complication, or administrative issue could still break a 99% thesis.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: Very high because the market closes April 16, 2026 and the key distinction is between dominant live position and formal winner declaration over the final rounds.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: High-quality primary evidence from FIDE plus useful but non-authoritative market context from Polymarket; enough for a confident directional view with residual caution.

key_assumptions: The market contract should be read literally as a future tournament-winner contract resolved by FIDE.
An elite Candidates field remains too competitive and variance-exposed for any one player to deserve near-99% odds absent special official circumstances.
No hidden official FIDE development has already made Sindarov nearly locked to win.
strongest_supports: Polymarket contract text says resolution is by the actual FIDE-declared winner, not by reputation or qualification status.
Chess.com contextual reporting confirms Sindarov won the 2025 FIDE World Cup and qualified for the Candidates, supporting a strong-favorite case but not near-certainty.
The key variant mechanism is structural: future multi-player elite tournaments have much more residual uncertainty than a 99% market implies.
strongest_disconfirmers: Sindarov's recent resume is exceptional: youngest-ever 2025 World Cup winner with clear elite momentum.
If there is hidden field-specific or format-specific official information, the current estimate could be materially too low.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: High: the contract closes on 2026-04-16 and the market is already priced near certainty, so checking whether the event is actually close to settled is central.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: Primary source-of-truth identification is solid via the contract and FIDE reference, but contextual evidence independence is only medium and official tournament-detail verification remained incomplete.

decision_summary.primary_crux: Sindarov is still overwhelmingly likely to win, but a 99.05% price is too aggressive without a clean official FIDE crosstable or tiebreak capture showing the exact late-stage state, so the residual risk is concentrated in verification fragility and the remaining rounds rather than broad field uncertainty.

causal_attribution.note: Evaluator distinguishes observed market/action facts from inferred causal attributions. Attribution population is intentionally left sparse until structured event + market-path reconstruction is implemented.
